projectDescription: A serverless censorship circumvention technique that intercepts local TLS traffic via MITM and re-forwards it using Domain Fronting through Xray-core, enabling access to blocked services like Google without a VPN server.
keyFeatures: Serverless censorship bypass — no VPN server required
MITM-based local TLS interception using self-signed certificates
Domain Fronting with fake SNI to reach blocked services
Cross-platform support: Windows, Linux, macOS, Android (no root)
Integrated into Xray-core v26.2.6+ as a native feature
Access to Google services (Meet, Drive, etc.) from restricted networks
Happy Eyeballs dual-stack connectivity for reliability
Ad blocking via geosite routing rules
publicApiSurface: mixed inbound (port 10808) — HTTP/SOCKS mixed proxy entry point
tls-decrypt-h11 inbound (port 11666) — HTTP/1.1 TLS MITM decryption
tls-decrypt-h211 inbound (port 11777) — HTTP/2 TLS MITM decryption
tls-repack-google-h11 outbound — Re-encrypt and domain-front for googlevideo.com
tls-repack-google-h211 outbound — Re-encrypt and domain-front for google services
fakedns — DNS strategy for traffic sniffing and routing
